CivBonuses.Name: Pou Whenua
CivBonuses.History: Pou whenua were carved boundary markers that signified tribal authority over land.
CivBonuses.Description: Can construct Pou Whenua, a unique territory marker that expands Māori territory beyond normal borders.

CivBonuses.Name: Taitamariki Warbands
CivBonuses.History: Young fighters were trained early and often fought in large, fast-moving groups.
CivBonuses.Description: Can train cheap, fast-producing Taitamariki infantry for rapid army production.

CivBonuses.Name: Wahine Toa Stone Throwers
CivBonuses.History: Women could participate in defence and support roles, including ranged combat when needed.
CivBonuses.Description: Access to cheap ranged stone-throwing units (Wahine Toa).

CivBonuses.Name: Toa Parau Skirmishers
CivBonuses.History: Skirmishing with spears and javelins was a key tactic in Māori warfare.
CivBonuses.Description: Javelin-throwing Toa Parau excel at ranged harassment and mobility.

CivBonuses.Name: Parekura Champions
CivBonuses.History: Elite warriors could hold the line against even the strongest enemy assaults.
CivBonuses.Description: Parekura act as heavily armoured frontline units, replacing traditional siege units in defensive warfare.
